title: T-062 — E2E: project create → advance G0..G2 → approve G3 with e-signature (NPD-f)
prompt:
# T-062 — Stage-Gate end-to-end

PRD: 01-NPD-PRD.md §17.6, §17.7
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Playwright E2E covering full project lifecycle: NPD Manager creates project → kanban view → tick required items → advance G0→G1→G2 → request G3 approval → e-sign with password → assert gate_approvals row + esign_hash + outbox events emitted in order.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/e2e/npd/stage-gate.spec.ts: login as NPD Manager → /(npd)/pipeline → click + new → fill wizard → submit → assert kanban shows new project at G0 → tick checklist items → drag to G1 → drag to G2 → click 'Request Approval' → e-sign with password → assert success.
2. Integration test apps/web/__tests__/integration/gate-events.test.ts: verifies outbox events emitted in order npd.project.created → npd.gate.advanced (×2) → npd.gate.approved.
3. Fixtures seed deterministic project state (no checklists pre-seeded).

## Files
- apps/web/e2e/npd/stage-gate.spec.ts [create]
- apps/web/__tests__/integration/gate-events.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/e2e/npd/fixtures/stage-gate-seed.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an NPD Manager session, when the user creates a project then advances it from G0 to G3 via the UI then approves with password, then a gate_approvals row exists with decision='approved' + non-null esign_hash + esigned_at per §17.6 e-sig flow
2. Given the project lifecycle completes, when the outbox table is queried, then events appear in order: npd.project.created → npd.gate.advanced (G0→G1) → npd.gate.advanced (G1→G2) → npd.gate.advanced (G2→G3) → npd.gate.approved per §17.6
3. Given an attempt to drag from G2 to G4 (non-adjacent), when drop fires, then the card snaps back and a toast 'Adjacency violation' appears per §17.12 adjacency guard

## Test strategy
- RED: write the 3 specs above before final wiring; run against isolated test container
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test:e2e stage-gate && pnpm --filter web test gate-events'

## Risk red lines
- Do not skip outbox event ordering assertion
- Do not run against shared/staging DB
- Do not embed real secrets in test password (use fixture)
